title: 10 minutes http client timeout is too low when image generation is done on CPU
summary:
### What is the issue?

[This line](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/x/imagegen/server.go#L58) sets the 10 minute timeout regardless of the hardware.

This is maybe fine on a GPU, but is certainly too low for a CPU.
The timeout should be at least an hour or more when CPU is used.

title: Stale stream timeout does not trigger fallback_providers chain
summary:
### Problem

When the primary provider becomes unresponsive during streaming (no chunks delivered within the stale timeout), Hermes kills the connection and retries with the **same** provider. It does **not** activate the `fallback_providers` chain.

This means that if a provider is alive but unresp

title: [PR] fix(snapshot): refuse restore --to existing destination; add --force opt-in
summary:
## Summary

`snapshot restore --to <dst>` used to overlay onto an existing destination's filesystem silently. Now it refuses by default; `--force` deletes the destination and recreates it from the source's image, with interactive confirmation unless `--yes` or `NEMOCLAW_NON_INTERACTIVE=1` is set.

On WSL2, `hasWindowsOllama` was determined solely by running `powershell.exe Get-Command ollama.exe`, which only returns a path when `ollama.exe` is on the calling user's PATH. Service-style installs (and any installer that does not update the user PATH) leave the binary undiscoverable b

title: [PR] feat(ng-conversations, MJExplorer): Agent Feedback app with 1-10 rating modal, consent flow, and owner-only enforcement
summary:

Adds an **Agent Feedback** application to MJ Explorer and reworks the

free-form comments**. Persistence happens directly on
`__mj.ConversationDetail.UserRating` / `UserFeedback`. Submitting a rating also

title: [PR] refactor(icons): move SVGs to node dirs, auto-tint monochromes

title: [PR] fix: skip active fingerprinters in passive phase of RunFingerprinters
summary:
## Summary

- `RunFingerprinters()` was running all registered fingerprinters against the root `/` response, including active fingerprinters designed to run against dedicated probe endpoints (e.g., `/_stcore/health`, `/api/2.0/mlflow/...`). This caused false positives when the root response happened
